articles.title: Aluminium: Chinese output above cap on price incentives – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank's FX & Commodity Analyst Volkmar Baur reports that Chinese Aluminium production has risen nearly 3% year-on-year and is running above the government’s annualized cap, supported by higher Aluminium prices and redirected Alumina flows as the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked.

articles.title: The USD is little changed vs major currencies - EUR, JPY and GBP - to kickstart the day
articles.expand: As North American traders enter the session, markets are relatively mixed. U.S. equities are showing modest divergence in pre-market trading, yields are little changed, and oil prices are higher by roughly $3. The USD initially moved higher before giving those gains back, and is now trading near unchanged levels. However, as is often the case ahead...

articles.title: Gold: Fed caution and strong Dollar cap upside – Commerzbank
articles.expand: Commerzbank’s Commodity Analyst Carsten Fritsch notes that Gold has fallen about 5% since the Iran war began, struggling to act as a safe haven as a stronger Dollar and repriced Fed expectations weigh.

articles.expand: AUD/USD trades around 0.7090 on Tuesday at the time of writing, up 0.25% on the day, rebounding after an initial pullback following the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) monetary policy decision.

articles.title: AUD: Bittersweet hike with stretched positioning – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Francesco Pesole notes the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) delivered a split 25 bp hike to 4.10%, initially read as dovish and triggering a sell-the-fact move in AUD/USD before a rebound on Governor Bullock’s comments.

articles.title: German ZEW collapses to -0.5 on rising stagflation risks
articles.expand: Germany’s ZEW Economic Sentiment plunged sharply in March, highlighting how rapidly confidence is deteriorating under the weight of the global energy shock. The expectations index collapsed from 58.3 to -0.5, far below market forecasts of 39.0, while Eurozone sentiment followed suit, dropping from 39.4 to -8.5. The primary driver is the escalation ...

articles.title: EUR/USD Price Forecast: Struggles to extend recovery above 1.1530
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair holds onto Monday’s gains slightly above 1.1500 during the European trading session on Tuesday. The major currency pair trades firmly as the US Dollar (USD) has come under pressure ahead of the Federal Reserve’s (Fed) monetary policy announcement on Wednesday.

articles.title: USD: Fed hike odds questioned as markets stabilise – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities analysts note that US rates rallied as markets stabilised, with attention on Fed policy expectations and geopolitical headlines. While hike odds have risen, they pushes back, arguing the hawkish outcome is more likely a prolonged pause.

articles.title: AUD/USD: RBA hikes keep Aussie supported – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Lee Hardman notes that the RBA delivered a second consecutive 25 bps hike to 4.10%, now the highest policy rate among G10 central banks, and that Australian Dollar gains versus the US Dollar were initially strong but faded.

articles.title: USD/INR remains firm as FIIs selling and Iran conflict weigh on Rupee
articles.expand: The Indian Rupee (INR) trades lower against the US Dollar (USD) on Tuesday. The USD/INR pair rebounds to near 92.90 after a correction on Monday as the Indian Rupee resumes its downside journey amid the continuous outflow of foreign funds from the Indian stock market.

articles.title: AUD: RBA split hike clouds terminal rate – Standard Chartered
articles.expand: Standard Chartered strategist Nicholas Chia notes that the Reserve Bank of Australia raised the cash rate to 4.10% in a 5-4 split decision, largely debating timing rather than direction.

articles.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Struggles to lure buyers despite hawkish RBA; Fed decision awaited
articles.expand: The AUD/USD pair struggles to capitalize on the previous day's goodish rebound from sub-0.7000 levels and seesaws between tepid gains/minor losses through the early European session on Tuesday.

articles.title: BoC: Policy hold as war risk lifts inflation – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank Strategist Molly Schwartz and Christian Lawrence expects the Bank of Canada (BoC) to keep its overnight rate at 2.25% at the March 18 meeting and through year-end, despite elevated inflation and weaker activity.

articles.title: USD/CAD Price Forecast: Consolidates around 1.3700 as focus shifts to Fed-BoC policy
articles.expand: The USD/CAD pair trades in a tight range around 1.3700 during the European trading session on Tuesday. The Loonie pair consolidates as investors await monetary policy announcements by the Bank of Canada (BoC) and the Federal Reserve (Fed) on Wednesday.

articles.title: Bank of Japan chief stresses need to hit 2% inflation backed by wage gains
articles.expand: TOKYO, March 17 : Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda said underlying inflation is accelerating toward the bank's 2 per cent target, stressing that price rises must be matched by solid wage gains.The remarks came ahead of the central bank's two-day policy meeting ending on Thursday, where the board is widely ex

articles.title: Japan's underlying inflation accelerating toward BOJ target, Governor Ueda says
articles.expand: TOKYO, March 17 : Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda said on Tuesday underlying inflation was gradually accelerating toward the central bank's 2 per cent target."We will guide monetary policy appropriately so that Japan sustainably and stably achieves our 2 per cent inflation target," Ueda told parliament.

articles.title: RBA set for second consecutive rate hike as Iran war fuels concerns over high inflation
articles.expand: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) is set to deliver another 25 basis points (bps) interest rate hike following its March monetary policy meeting on Tuesday, lifting the Official Cash Rate (OCR) to 4.10% from 3.85%.
